data.title: Trump Says 'Evil And Wickedness Will Not Prevail' In Easter Message, Chuck Schumer Calls Him 'Unhinged Madman' Over Controversial Iran Post
data.description: Speculators in the cryptocurrency market are eagerly anticipating President Donald Trump's upcoming conference with military officials. The odds of Trump mentioning "Iran" or "Iranian" more than 15 times stand at 74% on Polymarket. The highest probability words include "Power" or "Strength" at 85%, and "Pilots/Crew/Staff" at 67%. There is a 69% chance of him using words like "Hell" and 65% for "Obliteration" regarding military action in Iran. The likelihood of Trump saying "Make a Deal" is 65%, but no bets are placed on mentioning "Ceasefire." The prediction market for word mentions is gaining traction with Trump's speeches and press conferences being closely watched.

data.title: MEDIPEEL Gains Momentum in Korea's K-Pharmacy Channel
data.description: In Seoul, South Korea, as of April 5, 2026, K-Pharmacy has become a leading and quickly expanding retail option within Korea's beauty sector. Its credibility has risen significantly, with global consumers showing a growing interest in the brand. This growth highlights the increasing trust in K-Pharmacy, solidifying its position in the market.

data.title: What Will Trump Say During Press Conference With Military? Crypto Prediction Market Is Wagering on These Words
data.description: Bettors in the cryptocurrency world are actively speculating on President Trump's upcoming conference with military officials. There is significant interest in whether the speech will be de-escalatory, with high stakes related to Trump mentioning "Iran" or "Iranian" over 15 times, having odds of 74% on Polymarket. The likelihood of Trump using words like "Power" or "Strength" stands at 85%, while terms such as "Pilots/Crew/Staff" have a 67% chance. Words such as "Hell" and "Obliteration," previously used by Trump in reference to military actions in Iran, have odds of 69% and 65% respectively. The possibility of Trump mentioning "Make a Deal" is at 65%, but there are no bets placed on "Ceasefire." The word-mention prediction market is seeing a surge in activity as Trump's speeches and conferences draw attention.
